title: After-Action Report — Tier-1 EV texture + Tier-3 foot-speed infield hits
scope: Two follow-ons to the EV-texture work, both aimed at *real* batted-ball mechanics (the user explicitly deferred the park-quirk "gimmick" tier).

rationale.item: The borderline-grounder hit/out flex (prob.py) keyed off eye/contact/command only — foot speed was ignored, which is backwards for infield hits.

Arm-vs-speed was well covered on the outfield/baserunning side (_resolve_runner_advance / _resolve_table, SPEED_ADVANCE_MOD ±0.12, ARM_ADVANCE_MOD ±0.11, nickel '4th outfielder' power play) and left fully intact. What was missing was the infield leg-out: the throw to first on a grounder.
The new term is gated to the categorical grounder hit_types (ground_out, infield_single) on purpose — EV/LA aren't sampled until later in the PA flow, and you don't leg out a fly ball.

delegation_events.quote: the user explicitly deferred the park-quirk "gimmick" tier

delegation_events.decision: Sub-0.005 BABIP wobble accepted as not significant
delegation_events.quote: the user confirmed the sub-0.005 BABIP wobble is not significant

actions.description: Tier 1: five more EV/LA rules in the park hook (rules 10-14): can-of-corn (lazy fly LA 36-48, EV ≤ 88: single/double → fly_out), legged-out tapper (EV ≤ 62, low LA: ground_out → infield_single), frozen rope (EV ≥ 102, LA 12-26: single → double), down the line (|spray| ≥ 40, LA 8-30: single → double), wall-ball carom (EV ≥ 102 into tall ≥22ft deep ≥400ft alley: double → triple)

Tier 3: leg_out_dev = (batter.speed − avg_infield_arm) × INFIELD_HIT_SPEED_SCALE term applied only to infield grounder transitions (ground_out ↔ infield_single); new helper _avg_infield_arm() mirrors _avg_outfielder_arm()

Tuning knobs added to config: EV_TAPPER_MAX (62), EV_FROZEN (102), EV_LAZYFLY_P (0.45), EV_TAPPER_P (0.40), EV_FROZENROPE_P (0.18), EV_DOWNLINE_P (0.30), EV_WALLBALL_P (0.30), INFIELD_HIT_SPEED_SCALE (0.20)
New tests: 6 Tier-1 rule tests and 4 _avg_infield_arm tests

risk_posture: 10 (hit→out) and 11 (out→hit) pair off; 12-14 only move the extra-base mix.
fly_out / line_out / extra-base hits are deliberately untouched.
Mean-neutral across the league (symmetric in speed and arm); set to 0.0 for identity.

validation_claims.claim: Tier 1 fires as designed: 2B/BIP 0.198, 3B/BIP 0.018, 124 infield singles; hits/BIP within noise of the prior layer.
validation_claims.check_method: Deterministic rosters, sim 300, measure batted-ball rates

validation_claims.claim: Fast batters (speed ≥ 60) leg out infield singles at 0.0101/BIP vs 0.0050 for slow batters (≤ 40) — a clean ~2:1.
validation_claims.check_method: sim 300 and split infield-single rate by batter speed
Compute correlation on the sim output

validation_claims.claim: tests/test_stat_invariants.py — identical 5-fail/6-pass on baseline and on the new code (the 5 are pre-existing flask/full-season environmental failures); zero new invariant failures.
validation_claims.check_method: Run the invariant suite on baseline and new code

non_actions.item: Touching or gating the foot-speed-vs-outfielder-arm baserunning plays (first-to-third, scoring from second, bang-bang tags) or the nickel power play
non_actions.reason: those were already modeled and remain fully active
deferred as "gimmick" risk per the user

non_actions.item: Isolating the EV rolls onto a private RNG
non_actions.reason: same compatibility call as the prior AAR
set to sensible defaults and validated once at n=300

residual_risks: Probabilities not swept for an optimum; validated once at n=300.
open_hooks: INFIELD_HIT_SPEED_SCALE and Tier-1 probability knobs in o27/config.py; set to 0.0 for identity.
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Tier-1 EV texture + Tier-3 foot-speed infield hits
Why foot speed was the real gap (and why it's infield-only)
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/peaceful-thompson-ZB5UY`
**Scope line:** `o27/engine/park_effects.py`, `o27/engine/prob.py`, `o27/config.py`, `o27/tests/test_park_ev_texture.py`, `o27/tests/test_infield_arm.py`

Validation setup: "Deterministic rosters, `sim 300` (Tier 1) / `sim 300` (Tier 3)." A burner vs a weak-armed infield legs more out; a plodder vs rocket arms is rung up.
parser_notes: Clear AAR; Tier-1 rules table summarized into one action entry.
